summary: This brief index post introduces two pieces Joel wrote as blueprints for his company PaxDigita. The first is 'The Guerrilla Guide to Interviewing,' originally written for Microsoft and refined over his career. The second reflects on what made Microsoft a great workplace compared to his frustrating experience at Juno. Joel initially attributed the difference to an east coast/west coast cultural divide but concludes the real explanation is more nuanced.
key_points: Joel repurposed his Microsoft interviewing guide as the official PaxDigita hiring manual, showing how hiring practices transfer across companies
The contrast between Microsoft and Juno prompted reflection on what makes a company great to work for
Cultural differences in tech companies go beyond simple geographic stereotypes like east coast vs. west coast
money_quotes: Recently I've been thinking a lot about what made Microsoft a great company to work for, and why working at Juno was so frustrating.
At first I attributed the difference to the east coast/west coast thing, but I think it's a bit more subtle than that.
key_insight: What makes a company great or frustrating to work for is more nuanced than surface-level cultural or geographic explanations suggest.
